Pounds to Kilograms Converter (lbs to kg)

Convert pounds to kilograms in seconds with a precise, standards-based lbs to kg calculator.

Uses the international avoirdupois pound definition and SI kilogram standard.

Use the lbs to kg calculator

Enter a weight in pounds to instantly see the equivalent value in kilograms. The tool updates as you type and is optimized for mobile numeric input.

0
Weight @ gravity: –
Pro outputs

    How the pounds to kilograms conversion works

    Pounds and kilograms are both units of mass. Pounds are commonly used in the United States and a few other countries, while kilograms are the base unit of mass in the International System of Units.

    By international agreement, one avoirdupois pound is defined as exactly 0.45359237 kilograms. This fixed relationship gives the standard conversion factor between the two units.

    Formula for converting lbs to kg

    The general conversion formula is:

    kilograms = pounds * 0.45359237

    To go in the opposite direction, from kilograms to pounds, you can use the inverse factor: 

    pounds = kilograms / 0.45359237

    For quick mental estimates, many people round the factor to 0.45, but this introduces a small error. The calculator uses the exact factor 0.45359237 for accurate results.

    Real world example: body weight conversion

    Imagine you are reading a fitness plan that lists recommended body weights in kilograms, but your bathroom scale shows pounds. You want to know what 180 pounds is in kilograms.

    Apply the formula:

    kilograms = 180 * 0.45359237

    Multiplying gives approximately 81.65 kilograms. If you enter 180 lbs into the calculator, it will return the same value instantly, without you needing to do the math by hand.

    This type of conversion is also common in medicine, nutrition, and sports science, where dosage, energy expenditure, or performance tables are often given in kilograms.

    Practical tips for using lbs to kg conversions

    • For everyday use, rounding the result to one or two decimal places is usually enough. For example, 68.04 kg can be reported as 68.0 kg.
    • When converting for medical dosing or scientific work, keep more decimal places or follow the precision recommended by your institution.
    • Remember that weight shown on a scale can fluctuate during the day due to hydration and other factors, even though the conversion factor between pounds and kilograms is fixed.

    Pounds to kilograms: frequently asked questions

    How do you convert pounds to kilograms?

    To convert pounds to kilograms, multiply the weight in pounds by 0.45359237. For example, 150 pounds * 0.45359237 is about 68.04 kilograms.

    What is 1 pound in kilograms?

    One pound is exactly 0.45359237 kilograms according to the international avoirdupois pound definition.

    Why do scientists prefer kilograms instead of pounds?

    Kilograms are part of the International System of Units, which is the global standard in science and engineering. Using kilograms keeps measurements consistent and avoids confusion between different local units like pounds or stones.
